title: SAK reconciliation queue (sak-reconciliation-queue/v1)
description: Validates the committed SAK-RECONCILIATION-QUEUE.json. The reconciliation queue is the SINGLE queue shared between Wave A residue (files with WARN-level open issues after mechanical batch-remediate) and the Wave B parked queue (files the Refiner could not migrate after 3 retries). Two normative authorities are composed here: (1) plan 033 v7 § 14.17.3 — the original MONTHLY-REVIEW disposition queue with the four dispositions keep-as-is / manual-migrate / deprecate-skill / schema-revision-candidate; and (2) plan 048 v8 Delta 1.1 — which EXTENDS (does not replace) § 14.17.3 with a REAL-TIME ENFORCEMENT capability: a per-file state record { open_pr_state, wave_a_pending, wave_b_parked, target_version }, the `sak-wave-a-precedence` merge-gate (rule 2 mechanism), and the wave-B park (rule 3 mechanism). The monthly-review disposition function runs ALONGSIDE the real-time enforcement. This schema is consumed by scripts/sak-reconciliation-queue.py. DISTINCT from the SAK Phase-4 flip state (sak-state/v1) and the 13-entity runtime state machine (transition-table.v1.json, Blueprint B).

as_of.description: ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD) the queue contents were last refreshed.

items.description: The reconciliation-queue items. Each item is keyed (by convention) on `path`; the queue holds at most one item per path. Order is preservation order (enqueue order), not significance.

source.description: Where the item entered the reconciliation queue from (plan 033 § 14.17.3 — Wave A residue + Wave B parked share ONE queue). `wave-a-residue` = a file with WARN-level open issues after mechanical batch-remediate; `wave-b-parked` = a file the Refiner could not migrate after the § 14.17.2 3-retry ladder.

disposition.description: The monthly-review disposition (plan 033 § 14.17.3), reviewed at SAK AAR cadence (§ 14.13.2). `pending` = not yet dispositioned. The four real dispositions: `keep-as-is` (file stays valid at a lower tier; recorded in coverage map), `manual-migrate` (engineer manually edits; file lands in next batch), `deprecate-skill` (skill marked for removal in next CCP minor; consumers warned), `schema-revision-candidate` (routed to the § 14.A.3 schema-revision-candidates queue; schema may need revision).

openPrState.description: The per-file open-PR state (plan 048 v8 Delta 1.1). `open` = at least one contributor PR currently touches this file (refreshed from `gh pr list --search "<path>"` on each queue tick / pull_request webhook — the GitHub-state source is a STUB SEAM in the implementation). `none` = no open PR touches the file.

fileState.description: The per-file real-time state record (plan 048 v8 Delta 1.1). Carries the inputs the merge-gate (`wave_a_pending`) and the wave-B park (`open_pr_state` -> `wave_b_parked`) decide over. These are PURE inputs; the predicates that read them (merge-gate, wave-B-park-eligibility) live in scripts/sak-reconciliation-queue.py, and the GitHub/CI wiring is downstream.

wave_a_pending.description: True iff a mechanical wave-A op is queued for this file but has not yet landed. While true, the `sak-wave-a-precedence` required CI check is RED for the file, so a contributor PR touching it CANNOT merge until wave A lands and clears the flag (plan 048 v8 Delta 1.1 rule-2 mechanism).

wave_b_parked.description: True iff the wave-B (Refiner) dispatcher SKIPPED this file because it had an open PR (open_pr_state == open) and set the park flag (plan 048 v8 Delta 1.1 rule-3 mechanism). The pull_request:closed webhook (merged OR closed) clears this and re-enqueues the file on the next tick. Parked != failed (composes with § 14.17 durability).

target_version.description: The pinned kernel authoring contract version this file targets during the migration window = LATEST_PHASE_4_VERSION (plan 048 v8 Delta 1, recorded in migration-manifest.json). Authors never chase a moving schema.

queueItem.description: One reconciliation-queue item: a tracked SKILL.md path, where it entered from (source), its per-file real-time state record (file_state), and its monthly-review disposition (defaults to `pending`).

path.description: The SKILL.md path tracked by the migration. The queue holds at most one item per path.

enqueued_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp (date or date-time) the item was enqueued. Advisory; the queue's preservation order is the source of truth for ordering.

dispositioned_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp the disposition was assigned (set when disposition moves off `pending`).

note.description: Free-text rationale for the disposition or park (e.g. the WARN issue, the Refiner failure mode).
